summary: Good‑quality 1985 recording of a loosely tight, magical HJK show with standout Touch of Grey (despite a lyric slip) and Terrapin Station.
review: Fans who were there describe the night at HJK as loose yet vibey, highlighting a strong second set that featured a memorable Touch of Grey (even if Jerry flubbed a line) and a fiery Terrapin Station. No reviewer mentions any major recording flaws, so the sound is assumed to be solid for an ’85 source.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating of 4.2 across five reviews; overwhelmingly positive comments about the performance and no complaints about audio quality, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers do not discuss technical aspects; they simply note that the '85 sound is enjoyable, implying a decent capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The band was described as loosely tight with good vibes; the second set stood out, especially the Touch of Grey and Terrapin Station moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Flubbed a lyric in Touch of Grey, drawing audience titters.
